steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows the study's key finding: democratic strength restrained inflation only when paired with inequality. The conclusion must explain what this finding means for the Norway-Cameroon comparison that opened the passage — specifically, whether that comparison supports or undermines the idea that democratic institutions are bad at controlling inflation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the factors...have less to do with political institutions than with income inequality': too strong — the study shows inequality interacts with democratic strength; it doesn't eliminate institutions as a factor. ✗
  • Choice B — 'speculations...are based on measures that exaggerate inflation in democratic countries': introduces a methodology critique the passage never raises. ✗
  • Choice C — 'it would be a mistake to treat Norway/Cameroon inflation rates as indicative of an inherent shortcoming in democratic institutions': matches exactly — the study shows democracy can restrain inflation when inequality is present, so the two-country comparison doesn't prove democracy is an obstacle to inflation control. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Norway and Cameroon may have differed more on democratic strength than the indices showed': disputes index accuracy, which the passage never questions. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
